title: ErrorEnvelope
description: The operator-facing error format an adapter emits for a failure that needs human attention. The spec REQUIRES config-class failures (provider.class=config: codex no-worktree, bad model id, missing trust flag) to surface on the GitHub issue or as a dispatcher alert — NEVER log-only. `remediation` is mandatory: an error an operator cannot act on is NON-conformant. The `class` field mirrors the AdapterResult provider axis so the log-only prohibition (Clause E2) is machine-enforced rather than prose-only.

class.description: The provider-class this envelope reports, mirroring AdapterResult.provider.class. config/auth/quota all require operator action; transient is a logged-only blip. Defaults to config when omitted (the common envelope case is a config failure).

code.description: Stable, machine-readable error code (UPPER_SNAKE). e.g. CODEX_NO_WORKTREE, KIRO_AUTH_FAILED, AGY_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED, MODEL_UNKNOWN.

problem.description: One-line statement of what went wrong, operator-facing.

cause.description: The underlying cause / evidence (the sanitized reason string, e.g. "Antigravity 429: daily quota reached; resets in 33h48m45s").

remediation.description: The concrete action the operator MUST take (e.g. "run `kiro-cli login --use-device-flow` on the execution host"). MUST be present — an error envelope without remediation is NON-conformant.

doc.description: Optional pointer to the authoritative doc (an INV anchor or a docs/pipeline path).

surface.description: Where this envelope was surfaced. MUST be present. Clause E2: an operator-actionable class (config/auth/quota) MUST use issue-comment or dispatcher-alert, never log-only — the conditional below enforces this. log-only is permitted only for a transient class.

allOf.description: Clause E2: an operator-actionable class (config — the default when class is omitted — or auth/quota) MUST NOT be surfaced log-only. Only an explicit class=transient may be log-only.
